body_txt: It's often said that you shouldn't judge people without getting to know them. Despite there being multiple sayings emphasizing the importance of not judging people on their outer qualities, some people have no problem making assumptions about people based entirely on external qualities. Some of said qualities can include things like looks, race, the way someone dresses, background, or occupation. Other times, a person might only know a little information about someone and assume that what little they do know tells all they need to know about someone. Naturally, nine times out of ten, they’re wrong in their assumptions. The reason why a person might judge people so shallowly can vary; a bigoted person who might believe that every member of a group they are bigoted towards is a certain way, and assumes that everyone in that group is like that. People who are shallow in general will naturally judge people solely based on surface traits without bothering to get to know them. Shallow characters can judge different aspects of someone's character, such as their morality, intelligence, or monetary status. Even if they have never interacted with someone, a shallow person will assume either the worst or the best simply because of surface-level knowledge. An example of assuming the worst about someone would be assuming that an attractive person is promiscuous, stupid , and/or arrogant simply because they're attractive. An example of someone assuming the best about people would be believing that someone who wears glasses is intelligent simply because they wear glasses. This trope can be used in both sympathetic and unsympathetic ways. The former variant has a character start off as being shallow but eventually learn not to judge based on appearances. The unsympathetic variant will have people not growing from this trait, and are often contrasted with those who aren't shallow. This trope can also be used to create sympathy for a character by having them frequently be judged by shallow people who don't bother to get to know them. Super-Trope to Shallow Cannot Comprehend True Love and Loser Friend Puzzles Outsiders . Sub-Trope of Horrible Judge of Character . Someone who's guilty of Loving a Shadow will probably be guilty of this towards their crush while someone who Doesn't Know Their Lover might be guilty of this towards their romantic partner. Could lead to an In-Universe case of Ron the Death Eater or Draco in Leather Pants . Might lead to a Minor Flaw, Major Breakup . Someone who has the Face of a Thug might be a victim of these types of people. While this sadly happens in real life, because it's both very common, No Real Life Examples, Please!
